_comment: Canonical glyph palette for blink CORE. Codepoints from CaskaydiaMono Nerd Font (Unicode + box-drawing). These are the framework contract — they appear in every blink app and never change. Domain glyphs (mysql, docker, laravel, …) are NOT here: they are content, not contract. Apps register their own via registerGlyphs() — see README > ICONOGRAPHY > 'The glyph registry'.
registry_note: Domain glyphs are app content, not framework contract. blink ships NO domain glyphs registered in core. An app opts into glyphs at boot via registerGlyphs(...maps) and reads them back with glyph(name) / glyphColor(name), resolving through the same icon-set detection + {nerd → unicode → ascii} fallback as core glyphs. FOUR TIERS: (0) this contract, always on; (1) COMMON_DOMAINS, ~18 curated dev domains, opt-in; (2) category packs LANGUAGES/DATABASES/CLOUD/EDITORS/OS/COMPANIES, curated, opt-in; (3) a raw name→codepoint index of the whole Nerd Font resolved with nf(name) — no curated fallback/colour, a deliberate escape hatch. Registration accepts a verbose entry {nerd,unicode,ascii,color} OR an easy one {nf:'dev-laravel', color} (codepoint from the index, fallbacks+colour defaulted to ◆ / derived / --fg-muted). See ui_kits/tui_app/{glyphs.js, glyph-packs.js, glyph-index.js} and README > ICONOGRAPHY.
